svg.title: Sail-plan of the Spray, viewed from the port side. The drawing shows both the sail-plan of the boat that was in place during the two initial crossings of the Atlantic, and the modified sail-plan (after the bowsprit and boom were shortened and a jigger-sail was added astern of the wheel, turning the boat from a sloop into a yawl) used for the remainder of the voyage. The drawing includes the lengths of the mast, boom, gaff, and topping lift. A size scale measured in feet.
